creator: scottm@cs.ucla.edu
text:
- gcc dumps core at odd/random places during the compile process.
	- depend generates unreasonable line numbers (>200,000), and
	  appears caught in an infinite loop, thrashing on an "invalid"
	  dependency.

	System under reasonable load (LAVG == 3.53/4.02/3.10). Running
	in single user mode, doing a buildworld. Other utitilies,
	notably sendmail, exit with sig 11, at random times. No kernel
	panic generated (wish it would.)

Fix: 

Unknown. VM frobbing?
How-To-Repeat: 
	make CFLAGS="-O2 -m486 -pipe" -j 4 buildworld
